Title: 'Wrong in law': Human rights lawyer says footballers' fast-tracked citizenship is void from outset
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The grant of citizenship to seven naturalised footballers under Article 19 of the Federal Constitution by Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail is unconstitutional and unlawful, and cannot be dismissed as a mere procedural irregularity, a lawyer said.
Human rights lawyer Zaid Malek took issue with the
Enforcement Agency Integrity Commission's (EAIC) finding
that the home minister had the constitutional authority to grant citizenship to the seven players under Article 19(2) of the Federal Constitution, arguing that it overlooked the residency requirement under the same provision.
The EAIC yesterday found procedural irregularities in the citizenship applications of the seven naturalised footballers but maintained that the Federal Constitution empowers the home minister to consider such applications under Article 19, read together with Section 20(1)(e) of Part III of the Second Schedule to the Federal Constitution.
According to the commission, Section 20(1)(e) gives the minister discretionary authority to determine, on grounds generally or specifically specified by the minister, that a period during which a person resided outside the Federation may be counted as a period of residence in Malaysia.
However, Zaid argued that the minister's discretion could not override the residency requirement expressly stipulated in the Constitution, and that Section 20(1)(e) likewise did not permit that requirement to be waived.
"This conclusion is not grounded in law as it entirely ignores that Article 19(3) states that... they must first have resided in Malaysia to the aggregate amount of not less than 10 years within the 12 years preceding the date of the application.
"The grant of citizenship under Article 19 thus is unconstitutional and wrong in law, and cannot be whitewashed as simply a procedural irregularity," he said.
On whether the citizenships could be invalidated, Zaid said that if they were granted contrary to the Constitution, they should be treated as void from the outset.
He also cautioned that the EAIC's recommendations were not legally binding on the agencies concerned and that no legal recourse could be based solely on those recommendations.
Former Malaysian Bar president Salim Bashir said the grant of citizenship by naturalisation is generally an exclusive exercise of federal government power under Article 19, and that such decisions are ordinarily immune from judicial review.
"Generally, the decision to grant citizenship by naturalisation ousts jurisdiction and cannot be questioned in a court of law," he said.
However, Salim said the courts may still exercise jurisdiction over procedural errors committed during the naturalisation process, notwithstanding that general immunity.
Yesterday, the
EAIC found procedural irregularities
in the citizenship applications of the seven naturalised footballers, including an unusually fast assessment process and lapses in immigration and citizenship requirements.
Nevertheless, the commission said its investigation found that the Federal Constitution empowers the home minister to consider citizenship applications by naturalisation under Article 19, read together with Section 20(1)(e) of Part III of the Second Schedule to the Federal Constitution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yong Peng PH candidate flags alleged campaigning after deadline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Yong Peng state constituency, Yong Hui Yi, has lodged an official complaint with the Election Commission (EC), alleging that Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Ling Tian Soon continued campaigning after the campaign period ended.
Yong claimed that MCA Youth and Ling had continued campaigning on social media after the official campaign period ended, in breach of election regulations.
She said the campaign-related content was posted in the early hours of today.
"The video called on voters to cast their ballots for candidate number one for the Yong Peng state constituency (Ling) and also displayed a sample ballot paper.
"This clearly constitutes campaigning and an appeal for votes," she said in a Facebook post.
Yong said the actions of MCA Youth and Ling after the campaign period had ended amounted to a violation of election regulations and the EC's reminder.
She said the commission had issued a reminder yesterday that all campaign activities must stop before midnight on the eve of polling day, with campaigning through social media platforms including Facebook, X, Instagram and TikTok required to cease before 11.59pm on July 10.
Yong said she had submitted an official complaint to the EC and urged the authorities to conduct an immediate investigation and take appropriate action against any party found to have breached election rules.
"Elections must be conducted in a fair, transparent and integrity-driven environment. Any violation of election regulations should not be allowed to go unchecked," said the Johor DAP deputy publicity secretary.
New Straits Times has reached out to Ling and MCA Youth for comments.
Meanwhile, DAP's Kasthuri Patto has urged the EC and police to take action against another BN candidate, Nicole Tan, for allegedly violating election laws.
In a Facebook post, she claimed that Tan, the BN-MCA candidate for the Skudai state seat, had parked a car bearing campaign material stickers, which are prohibited on polling day, in front of SMK Taman Tun Aminah.
"This violates the law and constitutes an election offence. Please take action," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Article:

The election saw 172 candidates vying for the 56 state seats up for grabs.Stay tuned as the New Straits Times brings you live updates on the Johor election results.

Title: Johor polls: Perlis student braves 12-hour journey to cast vote
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 12-hour journey spanning hundreds of kilometres was no obstacle for first-time voter Aina Nazirah Rahmat, who travelled from Perlis to Johor to cast her ballot in the state election today.
The 21-year-old Diploma in Information Technology student at Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Polytechnic in Arau was determined to vote despite having to alter her travel plans when express bus tickets from Arau to Batu Pahat sold out.
Instead, she bought a ticket to Melaka, where her brother picked her up before continuing the journey to Batu Pahat.
"I was determined to return to Johor and vote even though the direct bus tickets to Batu Pahat were sold out. I managed to get a ticket to Melaka instead, and my brother picked me up there before we travelled home," she said today.
After arriving at her hometown in Parit Sulong at 8am, Aina Nazirah went straight to the polling centre at Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan Cina (SJKC) Tongkang Pechah to cast her vote for the Parit Yaani state constituency.
She said neither the long journey nor the heavy rain at the polling centre dampened her enthusiasm to fulfil her responsibility.
"I was excited to vote for the candidate I believe can serve the people well. I am relieved that I made it home to vote. As a first-time voter, this is a meaningful experience that I will cherish forever," she said. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Posthumous Penang state award honours Nordin Ahmad's enduring legacy
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: The posthumous conferment of the Darjah Setia Pangkuan Negeri (DSPN) on the late Datuk Nordin Ahmad, 55 years after his passing, honours the enduring legacy of the Penang-born legendary actor and recognises his significant contributions to Malaysia's film industry.
The late actor's nephew, Mohd Sabri Mohd Sidik, 60, said the award reflected the lasting impact of Nordin's contributions beyond acting, recognising his role as a director and scriptwriter who contributed significantly to the development of Malay cinema and elevated Penang's standing in the nation's arts scene.
He said Nordin, a native of Kampung Kota, Permatang Pasir, Permatang Pauh, was one of the leading figures of the golden era of Malay cinema, with his works continuing to serve as references and sources of inspiration for actors and researchers to this day.
"I would like to convey my sincere appreciation and gratitude to the Penang state government for conferring the DSPN posthumously on the late Nordin Ahmad.
"His contributions not only shaped the development of the country's film industry but also continue to inspire and serve as a reference for generations of actors and researchers to this day," he told reporters after representing Nordin's family to receive the award at the Penang State Awards, Medals and Honours Investiture Ceremony held in conjunction with the 85th birthday celebration of Penang Yang di-Pertua Negeri at Dewan Sri Pinang here today.
Sabri, an employee of the Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP), said the late Nordin Ahmad left an indelible mark on the local film industry as an actor, director and scriptwriter, with his portrayal of iconic Malay warrior characters becoming synonymous with his illustrious career.
He said the posthumous conferment of the DSPN, which carries the title "Datuk", was the first such honour received by the late actor, while his legacy had previously been immortalised on the National Film Development Corporation Malaysia (Finas) Walk of Fame as one of the legendary figures of Malay cinema.
Sabri said his family plans to display the DSPN medal at Galeri Karyawan Nordin Ahmad at SK Permatang Pasir, giving fans, researchers and the public an opportunity to see the honour bestowed upon the legendary film icon.
The late Nordin Ahmad died on June 15, 1971, at the age of 41 at Singapore General Hospital due to jaundice.
He left behind a remarkable legacy through numerous films that remain deeply appreciated by Malay film enthusiasts, including
Hang Tuah
,
Anak-ku Sazali
,
Sri Mersing
,
Semerah Padi
,
Air Mata Duyung
,
Sultan Mahmud Mangkat Dijulang
,
Gurindam Jiwa
and
Lanchang Kuning
.
The four-day Penang State Honours, Awards and Medals Investiture Ceremony, which began today and will continue on July 13, 14 and 15, saw 101 individuals conferred state honours, while another 1,120 recipients received stars and honorary decorations.
Meanwhile, DSPN recipient Vygon Global Director Datuk K. Thanapathy said he was deeply honoured and humbled to receive the state honour.
The award recognises his sustained contributions to the medical industry, both within Malaysia and across the wider region.
"I am deeply honoured and humbled to be conferred the title of Datuk, and I accept it with humility and a profound sense of responsibility.
"I would like to thank the state of Penang, the government, and relevant authorities for this recognition, as well as my family, colleagues and all those who have supported my journey in the medical industry and community service," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Onn Abu Bakar claims phantom voting at Senggarang polling centre
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan Senggarang state seat candidate Onn Abu Bakar claimed a phantom voting incident occurred at a polling centre in the constituency today.
He said the alleged incident was detected at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Senggarang at 10.30am.
Onn said he was informed by a voter that the individual was unable to cast his ballot because someone else had allegedly voted using his name.
Following the incident, Onn urged the Election Commission (EC) to ensure all eligible voters, particularly in the Senggarang constituency, are able to exercise their voting rights.
"I urge the public to vote as early as possible and ensure that your vote is secure and has not been used by someone else," he said in a video posted on his Facebook page.
Onn said the 25-year-old male voter lodged a police report at the Senggarang police station at 11am over the alleged incident to enable the authorities to carry out further investigations.
At press time, there has been no official response from the EC regarding the allegation. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Typhoon Bavi: 900,000 evacuated in China, millions on alert across East Asia
Article:
KEELUNG: More than 900,000 people have been evacuated in China as Typhoon Bavi brought heavy rain and strong winds to northern Taiwan and Japan's remote south-western islands, toppling trees and leaving tens of thousands without power.
Extreme weather has already caused widespread disruption in southern and central China this week, with storms killing at least 39 people, causing rivers to overflow and triggering the collapse of a reservoir dam.
Typhoon Bavi is expected to make landfall early on Sunday near Wenzhou, a city of nearly 10 million people in eastern Zhejiang province, where authorities said 887,801 people had been evacuated from their homes by late Friday.
"The proactive, all-out mobilisation, which is sparing no effort or cost, is undertaken entirely to guard against the (worst-case) scenario," Wenzhou authorities said in a statement.
Residents used wood to reinforce metal shutters protecting shops and taped windows as Bavi was forecast to bring "exceptionally heavy rains" to eastern Zhejiang and north-eastern Fujian province, CCTV footage showed.
Further north, torrential rain prompted the evacuation of more than 100,000 people as authorities increased water discharge from Beijing's Miyun Reservoir to prepare for possible flooding.
Streets were largely deserted in northern Taiwan, where most businesses remained closed for a second day as strong winds and rain battered the region.
More than 14,000 people have been evacuated, hundreds of flights cancelled and more than 170,000 households across the island affected by power outages due to the storm.
"Everyone is afraid of the severe weather and staying indoors, but I only came out because I have orders," a breakfast shop owner surnamed Tsai told AFP in Taiwan's port city of Keelung.
"Some people are on duty and wouldn't have anything to eat, so I still need to deliver food to them," the 50-year-old said.
Bavi was downgraded from a super typhoon as it moved across the Pacific Ocean after striking Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands on Monday.
Its maximum sustained wind speeds fell to 137kph, with gusts of about 173kph on Saturday, Taiwan's Central Weather Administration (CWA) said.
The CWA warned of "extremely torrential rain" across northern Taiwan and "dangerous waves" of up to 10 metres along the coast as Bavi passed north of the island.
In the Philippines, the death toll from landslides and other incidents triggered by heavy rain linked to Bavi rose to 18, with most fatalities reported on the southern island of Mindanao.
Nearly 11,000 people across the archipelago fled their homes, while dozens of ports remained closed, with 313 vessels taking shelter.
More than 18,000 households and facilities across Okinawa lost power as the typhoon struck Japan's remote south-western islands, with the Miyako region the worst affected.
Japanese airlines cancelled dozens of flights, affecting more than 26,000 passengers.
"Miyako is taking worst of typhoon Bavi," professional storm-chaser James Reynolds said in a post on X from Ishigaki on Saturday.
"Conditions on Ishigaki gale force with some overnight gusts to typhoon strength."
—AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Counting starts at 5pm, says EC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Election Commission (EC) said the counting of early votes for the Johor election will only begin at 5pm today.
In a Facebook post, the commission urged the public to rely only on
its official announcements
for verified information on the election results.
It also cautioned against spreading or believing false information, saying such actions could disrupt the smooth conduct of the election process.
Early voting was held on July 7, while polling took place today.
Earlier, graphics claiming that early vote counting was already underway circulated widely on social media.
More than 2.7 million voters are eligible to elect representatives for the 56 state seats being contested by 172 candidates at 1,076 polling centres across Johor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Armed forces chief's Cambodia visit reinforces military ties
Article:
PHNOM PENH: Armed forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman's visit to Cambodia this week is expected to further strengthen military ties between the two countries.
The visiting general held high-level talks with his Cambodian counterpart and government leaders.
During the meetings, both sides reaffirmed their commitment to enhancing defence cooperation to maintain peace and stability in Southeast Asia.
In a Facebook post today, Malek Razak said Malaysia and Cambodia were committed to elevating longstanding defence partnership through closer strategic cooperation and sustained high-level engagement.
"As Asean member states with a common vision for a peaceful, secure and prosperous region, we reaffirmed the importance of strengthening mutual understanding, enhancing institutional collaboration and fostering greater cooperation between our armed forces," he said.
Earlier on July 9, he held bilateral talks with the Commander-in-Chief of the Royal Cambodian Armed Forces, General Vong Pisen.
As part of his visit, Malek Razak also travelled to the Cambodia–Thailand border in Banteay Meanchey province to assess the security situation.
He later met Cambodian Deputy Prime Minister and National Defence Minister Tea Seiha on Friday.
Cambodia's state media,
Agence Kampuchea Presse
, reported that on Saturday morning, Malek Razak paid a courtesy call on former prime minister Hun Sen, who is also the current President of Cambodia's Senate.
During a series of bilateral meetings, Malaysia and Cambodia discussed broadening defence collaboration by implementing existing bilateral frameworks, expanding military exchanges, professional military education, training opportunities and capacity-building initiatives.
"We also emphasised the value of maintaining close cooperation within Asean-led defence platforms to address emerging security challenges and reinforce regional stability," said Malek Razak in his post.
The defence chief is leading a Malaysian delegation to Cambodia at the invitation of Vong Pisen from July 8 to 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Voter turnout hits 63.13pct with two hours to go
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Voter turnout for the Johor election reached 63.13 per cent as at 4pm today.
The Election Commission, in its hourly update, said 1,693,625 of over 2.7 million eligible ordinary voters had cast their ballots.
Early voting on Tuesday recorded a turnout of 95 per cent, with 19,471 voters casting their ballots.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Typhoon Bavi hits Taiwan, most areas suspend work and classes
Article:
TAIPEI: Typhoon Bavi, the ninth typhoon of the year, reached north-eastern Taiwan early today, forcing flight cancellations in Taipei and Taichung and the suspension of work and classes across most parts of the island.
The storm brought intensifying winds and rainfall, with scattered reports of damage including power outages and a landslide, according to Xinhua.
Weather authorities said the centre of the typhoon was located over waters about 360km south-east of Taipei, moving north-west at a speed of 27kph.
Heavy rain was expected to continue throughout the day, with extremely heavy rainfall forecast in mountainous areas north of Taichung, while strong winds were reported in many areas.
The public was advised to avoid beaches due to high waves.
Meteorological officials said rainfall was expected to continue into the night in mountainous areas of central and southern Taiwan, before gradually easing on Sunday.
—Bernama-Xinhua
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad quits Bersatu
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad has resigned as Bersatu Tampin division chief and withdrawn his membership from the party, effective today.
"I, Ridzuan Ahmad, hereby tender my resignation as Tampin Bersatu division chief, effective this Saturday, 11 July 2026," he said in the letter addressed to party secretary-general Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali.
He added that his resignation from the division chief post would be accompanied by a full withdrawal from party membership, taking effect on the same date.
Ridzuan said the decision was made after careful deliberation, and extended his gratitude to the party leadership, division members and all those who had extended their cooperation, support and trust to him throughout his tenure.
"I pray that the party continues to progress and succeed in the future," he said.
[[nid:1486122]]
Yesterday,
Aziman Hadi Nazri
said he had stepped down as a Bersatu Armada National executive council member and Supreme Council member.
Earlier, Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah confirmed that he had resigned as an MPT member to focus on administering the state government.
Last week, Sena assemblyman and Perlis executive councillor Marzita Mansor also submitted her resignation from the party leadership.
On Thursday, Bersatu Perlis secretary Azahar Hassan announced that he had resigned from the party with immediate effect and relinquished all positions he held in Bersatu.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao link opens, easing border congestion
Article:
BUKIT KAYU HITAM: A new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah with Thailand's Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex opened to the public today, aimed at easing congestion and improving cross-border movement.
The route, which opened at 6am, is expected to facilitate travel between Malaysia and Thailand, particularly during weekends and public holidays when traffic congestion is usually heavier at the border checkpoint.
A Bernama check found traffic along the route was smooth today, with most users welcoming the opening of the new link.
Motorist Mohd Faizal Ahmad, 42, from Penang, said the new route was better organised and provided greater convenience for Malaysians travelling to Thailand.
"Previously, traffic congestion was quite severe, especially during the school holidays. With this new route, I believe it will help reduce congestion and shorten travel time," he said when met here today.
Lorry driver Wan Muhammad Shahid Wan Mohd Desa, 28, said the new road was wider and separated heavy and light vehicles more systematically when entering the Sadao CIQ, allowing for smoother traffic flow.
"Previously, heavy vehicles travelling from the Bukit Kayu Hitam ICQS to Danok shared the same route with other vehicles, resulting in severe congestion for traffic entering and leaving Thailand. We hope the new route will help reduce the problem," he said.
Thai national Mat Li Daman, 59, who frequently travels to Malaysia, said the new Sadao CIQ was more spacious and comfortable, with improved facilities for documentation procedures, including vehicle entry declarations.
"The new building is much larger than the old, cramped checkpoint. I hope these facilities will benefit the people of both countries, who have long lived as neighbours," he said.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his Thai counterpart Anutin Charnvirakul officiated the opening of the new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam ICQS with the Sadao CIQ.
The project was developed to improve the efficiency of border operations, facilitate the movement of people and goods, and boost cross-border trade and tourism between Malaysia and Thailand.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Busted in basement: Immigration smashes 'vault-like' Cheras vice den with steel doors
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A syndicate went to extraordinary lengths to shield its suspected vice operation, turning an underground karaoke centre in Cheras into a "vault" fitted with multiple steel doors and escape routes to evade enforcement action.
The raid on the premises in Taman Len Seng, Cheras, which began at 1am today, found the unlicensed karaoke centre operating from the basement car park of a luxury residential building. It was also equipped with closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras at every entrance.
Personnel from the Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department had to use covert methods to gain access to the exclusive club, which admitted only regular customers seeking entertainment with guest relations officers (GROs).
Kuala Lumpur Immigration director Hamsha Injau said initial investigations found that the premises charged up to RM1,300 for a six-hour session, which included the room, alcoholic beverages and GRO services.
"The raid was conducted following intelligence gathering after we received public complaints about business activities at the location, as well as the presence of many foreigners visiting the premises.
"Based on our initial investigations, we believe the foreign women were working voluntarily as GROs at the premises. We also believe the premises had been operating for only about two months," he said.
The operation ended at about 4am, with 52 people detained, including four Malaysians — three men believed to be the caretakers and managers of the premises, and a woman suspected of managing the GROs.
The remaining detainees were foreigners, comprising 33 Vietnamese women and one Thai woman believed to be working as GROs, as well as 10 Bangladeshi men, four Myanmar men and one Chinese man employed at the premises.
Checks at the location found 14 operating karaoke rooms, with several more under construction in a concealed section of the premises.
The premises also had several rear exits that served as escape routes during enforcement raids, while three layers of steel doors at the main entrance were specially designed to delay enforcement officers and give customers and GROs time to flee.
However, the enforcement team detected the escape routes early and placed them under close surveillance, preventing anyone from escaping.
A local woman in her 50s, believed to be the "mummy" managing the foreign GROs, denied any involvement and claimed the women had come to the premises of their own accord.
"I did not bring them here. They came on their own, maybe with their boyfriends. I do not know what they do inside. I only serve customers who come in.
"I do not know how much they are paid. What they do inside the rooms has nothing to do with me. I only work here. I do not take care of them (GROs)," she said.
However, several customers denied bringing the women into the karaoke rooms and admitted the foreign GROs were provided by the premises.
The detained GROs claimed during questioning that they could not speak either Malay or English.
Hamsha said all the foreign and local individuals involved had been detained, while 19 local customers were issued witness summonses to appear at the Kuala Lumpur Immigration Department to assist investigations under the Immigration Act 1959/63 and Immigration Regulations 1963.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shorts allowed at polling stations, EC clarifies after viral complaint
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Election Commission (EC) today clarified that voters are allowed to wear shorts at polling stations after a man claimed he and his family were denied entry to cast their ballots at a polling centre in Skudai.
EC deputy chairman Dr Azmi Sharom said there was no official dress code prohibiting voters from wearing shorts or slippers when casting their votes.
"The only restriction is clothing displaying a political party logo or a candidate's image. There is no such dress code," he said.
The clarification came after a Facebook post alleging that a family was denied entry to the polling station at SMK Taman Tun Aminah in Skudai went viral.
In the post, the voter claimed that he, his brother who had returned from Singapore, and other family members had gone to the polling centre to
vote.
He alleged that at about 8am, security personnel stopped them from entering because they were wearing shorts, questioned whether they were aware of the dress requirements, and asked them to leave.
The voter claimed he later found that other voters wearing shorts had been allowed to cast their ballots.
He said he confronted the personnel involved after voting and recorded the exchange before uploading it online, alleging that he and his family had been treated unfairly.
The post showed a man in a yellow T-shirt and khaki shorts confronting a security guard outside the school gate, where a notice listing the school's visitor dress code stated that shorts, sleeveless shirts and slippers were prohibited.
However, the EC said election regulations only prohibit voters from wearing clothing, face masks or accessories bearing political party logos or candidate branding within 50 metres of polling stations.
Polling for the Johor election began at 8am today and will continue until 6pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Opposition mayor among 36 detained in Turkey graft probe
Article:
ISTANBUL: Turkish prosecutors have ordered the detention of 36 people, including the mayor of an Ankara district controlled by the main opposition party, as part of an investigation into alleged bribery and tender-rigging, the Ankara chief prosecutor's office said today.
Authorities have detained 27 of the suspects, while searches continue for the remaining individuals, the prosecutor's office said in a statement.
Huseyin Can Guner, mayor of Ankara's Cankaya district and a representative of the Republican People's Party (CHP), said in a post on X that he had informed authorities of his whereabouts and provided officers with a spare key to his home while he travelled to Ankara.
"Since taking office, we have managed this institution in the best possible way and have not engaged in the slightest conduct that would embarrass anyone who has placed their trust in us," Guner said.
The operation follows a series of investigations into municipalities run by the CHP.
The government has maintained that the judiciary is independent, while the CHP has rejected the allegations and accused authorities of pursuing politically motivated investigations.
—Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kiandee asks Muhyiddin if Bersatu Supreme Council still has quorum
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Suspended Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ronald Kiandee has questioned whether the party's Supreme Council still meets the required
quorum to hold valid
meetings following the resignation, suspension and removal of several council members.
Kiandee, who is Beluran member of parliament, said Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's assurance that the party's internal crisis would be resolved internally did not address the key issue of whether the Supreme Council still met the quorum requirement under Clause 13.12 of the party constitution to function and hold valid meetings.
He said the clause requires the attendance of more than half of the council's total membership for its meetings to be valid.
"The resignations reflect a loss of confidence in the president's leadership, and any failure to maintain the required quorum would cast doubt on the legitimacy of decisions made by the council.
"Bersatu risks being left in a state of administrative limbo, leaving it incapable of making decisions that are valid under the party's constitution," he said in a statement today.
Kiandee said the crisis would eventually require the intervention of the Registrar of Societies, affecting Bersatu's standing and potentially disrupting Perikatan Nasional's preparations for the Negeri Sembilan state election.
"The PN Supreme Council must meet urgently to assess Bersatu's position within the coalition and ensure the alliance's stability, credibility and election readiness are not undermined by the party's internal crisis.
"The interests of PN must take precedence over those of any individual, as a prolonged crisis without a resolution would undermine public confidence in the coalition's ability to provide stable, credible and principled leadership," said Kiandee, who was suspended from the party for two election terms on March 10.
Earlier today,
another Supreme Council member resigned
from the party, relinquishing all his positions with immediate effect.
Aziman Hadi Nazri said he had stepped down as a Bersatu Armada National executive council member and Supreme Council member.
Yesterday, Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah confirmed that he had
resigned as a Supreme Council
member to focus on administering the state government.
Last week, Sena assemblyman and Perlis executive councillor Marzita Mansor also submitted her resignation from the party leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hundreds of thousands evacuated as Typhoon Bavi approaches China
Article:
WENZHOU: China evacuated more than 600,000 people on Saturday ahead of Typhoon Bavi's expected landfall near the eastern city of Wenzhou, after the storm brought heavy rain and strong winds to Japan's southern Sakishima island chain and brushed past northern Taiwan.
Although Bavi has slowed and weakened as it moves north-west over cooler waters, the typhoon remains a significant threat due to the large amount of moisture carried within its rain bands, which stretch across an area roughly the size of France.
Bavi is forecast to make landfall near Wenzhou, a city home to about 10 million people, in the early hours of Sunday.
State media reported that more than 500,000 people had been evacuated in Zhejiang province, where Wenzhou is located, while more than 100,000 others were evacuated in neighbouring Fujian province.
"I'm a little worried, but I think it'll be OK. We've been through typhoons before. We'll get through it," said Wenzhou resident Huang Xinghuan, 50, who was buying groceries at a traditional wet market before it closed ahead of the storm.
Huang said his family had stocked up on two to three days' worth of water.
"I think supplies are well guaranteed now. There's no need to panic or stockpile a lot of food or other supplies," he added.
While Japan and Taiwan have not reported any deaths from Bavi so far, 17 people were killed in the Philippines due to heavy rain brought by an enhanced southwest monsoon worsened by the typhoon's impact.
Taiwan's fire department said 87 people had been injured, mostly due to falls from motorcycles or bicycles, falls, or being struck by objects.
In Taiwan, authorities evacuated more than 14,000 people, mainly from mountainous areas, as the island prepared for the typhoon's approach from the north.
Although Bavi did not make landfall in Taiwan, authorities took precautionary measures amid forecasts of up to 1 metre of rain in some areas.
Most evacuees were from northern and eastern Taiwan, while 920 international flights were cancelled, effectively shutting down operations at Taoyuan International Airport outside Taipei. All 282 domestic flights were also cancelled.
Almost all cities and counties across Taiwan declared a typhoon holiday on Saturday, closing offices and schools that would normally have operated during the weekend.
The main north-south high-speed rail service continued to operate, although with reduced frequency.
In downtown Taipei, some residents were still out despite strong winds and rain.
"It's OK, it's not that serious. It's just a little bit more wind," said resident Yeh Mao-hsiung, 68, who was out for a morning walk with his dog.
However, in Taipei's Beitou neighbourhood at the foothills surrounding the city, gusts of about 100kph brought down trees and caused rivers to swell.
In Wenzhou, Chen Qiuqin, in her 60s, walked through steady rain to her parents' home to help them prepare for the approaching typhoon, but said she was not overly concerned due to the government's preparations.
"I was worried about the flowerpots on my mother's balcony, so I'm going to help move them inside. My parents are both elderly and they're home alone, so I wasn't at ease," she said.
—Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Turkiye fines over 100 doctors for Caesarian deliveries
Article:
ANKARA: Turkiye's health ministry has fined more than 100 obstetrician-gynaecologists for carrying out Caesarian sections, suspending them from duty and forcing them to undergo training, BirGun newspaper reported on Saturday.
The country has the highest rate of C-section births among the OECD's 38 nations, according to the last available data from 2023, with around 615 such procedures out of every 1,000 live births that year.
Medical professionals have told AFP C-sections were more time-efficient for medical staff -- 30 minutes, versus 12 hours for a traditional delivery -- and lowered the risk of legal action over complications, ensuring a guarantee of safety for both the physician and the women.
Last year, the Turkish government began a campaign to tackle falling birthrates under President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's "Decade of the Family" initiative that has seen it move to exert greater control over how women give birth.
Erdogan, a pious Muslim who is pushing for women to have so-called natural births, wants to address Turkey's record number of elective C-section births, with his government in April 2025 banning such procedures at private healthcare facilities without medical justification.
According to BirGun, more than 100 doctors have been fined for performed C-sections, citing figures provided by medical associations across the country, sparking a backlash from heathcare professionals.
On its website, the Antalya Chamber of Physicians said obstetricians had been "issued with warnings, subjected to disciplinary investigations, temporarily suspended from practising, and compelled to attend antenatal training courses, on the grounds of high caesarean section rates across the country".
The Diken news website cited the case of one obstetrician working at a private hospital in Sakarya near Istanbul, who was dismissed at the request of the health ministry on grounds of a high rate of C-sections, then suspended for six months.
Over that period, the doctor would need to undergo training at a state hospital and would then sit an exam, and only if successful, would be able to resume practising medicine, it said.
Dr Ayse Gultekingil, a top official at the Turkish Medical Association (TTB), told BirGun that penalising doctors would not solve the problem of Turkiye's high Caesarian rate, which was "structural".
"Turkiye's caesarean birth rate exceeds 60 per cent. But the method of delivery reflects various problems within Turkiye's healthcare system," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fatal RXZ convoy crash: Motorcyclists were racing, say police
Article:
KUANTAN: Several motorcyclists in a Yamaha RXZ convoy involved in a fatal crash along the East Coast Highway near Jabor were racing, police said.
Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said preliminary investigations indicated this, adding that more than 30 motorcycles were involved in the crash.
Four people aged between 24 and 40 were killed and several others injured when the convoy collided with a car.
Ashari said police were examining dashcam footage showing the group performing dangerous stunts before the crash.
A one-minute video widely shared on social media showed several RXZ riders performing the "superman" stunt on the highway.
The crash occurred when a motorcycle rammed into the back of a car in the right lane, triggering a chain reaction as other riders failed to evade the collision and crashed into one another.
All the vehicles were heading from Kuantan towards Kemaman, Terengganu.
Eighteen injured victims were sent to Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital, while two others were taken to the Sultan Ahmad Shah Medical Centre @ IIUM for treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Abu Bakar to Shukri: Don't provoke political tension in Perlis
Article:
KANGAR: Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah has advised former menteri besar Mohd Shukri Ramli against issuing statements that could trigger political tension in the state.
He said the advice followed speculation that Shukri intends to table a motion of no confidence against his leadership at the upcoming Perlis state legislative assembly sitting.
Abu Bakar said any such move would be deemed contrary to the decree of the Raja Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, and warned that provocative statements on the matter could have legal implications under the Sedition Act, particularly in relation to 3R (race, religion and royalty) issues.
"The whole of Malaysia, and even the world, knows Tuanku's decree during the previous Perlis Dun (state) sitting.
"If he dares to table such a motion, it means he is going against Tuanku's decree.
"Therefore, I advise the former menteri besar not to make statements that could further escalate tensions," he said when met here today.
Shukri sparked speculation after posting a message on Facebook that read: "A traitor remains a traitor. We will continue to reject them wherever they are."
The post was uploaded a day after Abu Bakar announced his resignation as a member of the Bersatu Supreme Council.
Beseri assemblyman Haziq Asyraf Dun also shared a social media post believed to carry a similar message.
"A snake remains a snake even after shedding its skin. Betrayal remains etched as betrayal regardless of where they are!" he wrote.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK police release man arrested on suspicion of killing former minister Widdecombe
Article:
LONDON: A 26-year-old man arrested on suspicion of the murder of former British government minister Ann Widdecombe has been released and is no longer part of the investigation, police said on Saturday.
"Our priority remains identifying those responsible and ensuring that all available evidence is thoroughly examined," Assistant Chief Constable Matt Longman said in a statement released by Devon and Cornwall police.
"Our murder enquiry is in its early stages but moving at a significant pace. We are deploying all of the necessary resources to find out exactly what has happened," he added.
Ambulance workers called police to Widdecombe's home in rural southwest England on Thursday, where she had been found dead after suffering serious injuries, police said.
Widdecombe, 78, was known for her socially conservative views, first as a junior minister in Conservative prime minister John Major's 1992-1997 government and latterly as an immigration and justice spokesperson for Nigel Farage's populist Reform UK.
News on Friday of her death led to tributes from across the political spectrum in Britain, including from Prime Minister Keir Starmer. — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Foreign worker killed in Kapar iron factory boiler fire
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A foreign factory worker was killed in a boiler fire at an iron processing plant in Kapar, Klang, this morning.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters were alerted to the incident at 8.18am and immediately dispatched to the scene.
"The operations commander reported that the fire involved a 20ft by 20ft boiler at the iron processing factory, but the blaze had already been extinguished by factory workers before firefighters arrived.
"The 30-year-old victim was found with severe burns over his entire body and was pronounced dead at the scene by paramedics," he said in a statement today.
Ashrul Riezal said the cause of the fire and the estimated losses were still under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Isham Jalil applies to join Pas three years after Umno sacking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Supreme Council member Isham Jalil has applied to join Pas, three years after he was sacked from the party.
In a Facebook post today, Isham said he submitted his membership application yesterday.
"Taking the blessings of Friday, I completed and submitted my application to become a Pas member.
"After three years without belonging to any political party, I have decided to continue my struggle with Pas," he said.
In December 2023,
Isham was sacked
from Umno months after his vocal criticism of the party's leadership and its alliance with DAP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOF expands STR cash payments to reach remote Orang Asli communities
Article:
CAMERON HIGHLANDS: The Ministry of Finance (MOF) plans to conduct 10 Rahmah Cash Assistance (STR) payment programmes for Orang Asli communities in remote areas across Peninsular Malaysia to ensure recipients without bank accounts continue to receive government aid.
Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican said the programme held in Cameron Highlands today was the sixth conducted this year under the initiative, which was introduced in 2024 to expand access to financial assistance and government services in remote communities.
He said the Cameron Highlands programme was the first of its kind in the district and part of the 10 locations selected across Peninsular Malaysia.
"This programme is specifically organised for STR recipients who do not have bank accounts so they can continue receiving government assistance in cash, in line with the government's aspiration of ensuring no one is left behind," he said.
Johan told reporters this after visiting the programme at Dewan Datuk Mohd Nor @ Bah Ayob in Kampung Orang Asli Menson here today, where 113 Orang Asli recipients received STR payments totalling RM60,000.
Also present were Dewan Rakyat deputy speaker, who is also Cameron Highlands MP Datuk Dr Ramli Mohd Nor, and Cameron Highlands district officer Kamarul Iskandar Nordin.
Besides cash payments, the programme brought together various government agencies to provide services at a single location, including MyKad and birth certificate registration by the National Registration Department (JPN), Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) assistance, the opening of Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) accounts and the MySalam protection scheme.
Johan said similar STR payment programmes for Orang Asli communities in Sabah and Sarawak had been completed last month, covering 37 locations, before the initiative was expanded to remote areas in Peninsular Malaysia.
He added that the government was strengthening access to financial services in rural areas by deploying four additional mobile banking units this year, while encouraging recipients to open bank accounts to facilitate future aid disbursements.
He said a premises in an Orang Asli settlement in Cameron Highlands had also been registered as a MyKasih partner, bringing the number of Sara partner outlets in the district to nearly 50, including two in Orang Asli settlements.
Meanwhile, Ramli said the programme had been well received by the Orang Asli community as it not only facilitated the distribution of cash assistance but also enabled them to access multiple government services, including obtaining identity documents, at one location.
He said the initiative, involving MOF, BSN, the Department of Orang Asli Development (Jakoa), the Inland Revenue Board (LHDN) and JPN, demonstrated the government's efforts to ensure assistance and essential services reached communities in remote areas.
"Many Orang Asli are unable to access assistance through the banking system. By bringing together the relevant agencies, this programme ensures the aid reaches them more efficiently.
"It also enables JPN to identify Orang Asli who do not have essential documents such as birth certificates and identity cards, so the necessary applications can be processed as quickly as possible," he said.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sekinchan padi field turns scene of rare funnel cloud sighting
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A funnel cloud was spotted over a padi field along Jalan Tali Air 4, Sekinchan near here this morning.
Selangor Tourism and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim said preliminary information indicated that the phenomenon lasted between three and four minutes and occurred at about 11.40am.
"The whirlwind formed in the middle of a padi field near the Sekinchan Terminal. Had it occurred in a residential or village area, it would certainly have caused significant losses.
"Fortunately, it happened away from populated areas, so there was no damage. It was simply a unique and unusual phenomenon," he told Bernama when contacted today.
Ng, who is also Sekinchan assemblyman, was commenting on a 57-second video circulating on social media, showing the funnel cloud over the padi field. The footage was recorded by residents in the vicinity of Jalan Tali Air 4.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan fire dept to step up homestay, budget hotel fire safety checks
Article:
TUMPAT: The Kelantan Fire and Rescue Department will intensify inspections of homestays, budget hotels and guesthouses across the state this year to ensure they comply with fire safety requirements.
Its director Farhan Sufyan Borhan said the focus would be on homestays, as many were believed to be operating without registration, making fire safety monitoring more challenging.
"We will study information from various online booking platforms such as Booking.com and Agoda to identify premises operating as homestays and ensure they comply with fire safety requirements.
"So far, there have been no fires involving homestay guests in the state. However, the department is taking proactive measures by warning owners to meet fire safety requirements before any incident occurs," he told reporters at the Fire Safety Campaign and Community Fire Point programme in Pulau Teluk Renjuna today.
He said homestay operators should, at the very least, provide fire extinguishers and display instructions on their proper use to allow guests to respond quickly in the event of a fire.
He said fire extinguishers were a basic requirement that could help contain a blaze during its early stages before firefighters arrived.
On enforcement, Farhan said the department would conduct field inspections to identify premises operating as homestays, although some owners had been uncooperative.
"In fact, the registration of homestays is not under the Fire Department's jurisdiction. Our responsibility is to ensure premises converted from residential homes into homestays comply with fire safety requirements," he said.
He said the department would first provide advice to owners before issuing a fire hazard abatement notice (MBK) if premises were found to have failed to meet the required standards.
Meanwhile, Farhan said the department had achieved 130 per cent of its inspection target for budget hotels and homestays, but inspection efforts would continue to be intensified due to the growing number of accommodation premises in the state.
In a separate development, he said the department had received 2,190 fire-related emergency calls from January to date, with the highest number recorded in March and April.
He added that this was the second-highest number of fire calls in Malaysia after Kedah, and expressed hope that fire incidents in the state could be reduced for the remainder of the year.
—Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Five polling centres closed as of 2pm
Article:
MERSING: Balai Raya Pulau Besar became the first polling centre to close in today's Johor election.
Tenggaroh state constituency returning officer Jamil Hasni Abdullah said the centre, which had one polling stream and 32 registered voters, closed at 11am.
"The polling centres at Balai Raya Pulau Aur and SK Pulau Pemanggil closed at noon, while Balai Raya Pulau Sibu and SK Pulau Tinggi closed at 2pm," he said.
The five polling centres across the islands serve a total of 690 registered voters.
The Election Commission reported that 56.77 per cent, or 1,522,961, of the state's more than 2.7 million registered voters had cast their ballots as of 3pm.
Polling across the state is scheduled to continue until 6pm today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari Ghani: Malaysia, Finland explore circular economy cooperation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia and Finland are exploring closer cooperation on circular economy implementation, with Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani saying the country's network of industrial parks could serve as a practical starting point for sustainable industrial development.
Johari said this following a courtesy call from Finnish Ambassador to Malaysia Anne Vasara, during which both sides discussed opportunities to advance sustainable and competitive economic development through bilateral cooperation.
"Our discussion focused on the potential to collaborate on implementing a circular economy model in Malaysia, in line with our commitments to sustainability and climate action," he said in a Facebook post.
He said areas of cooperation discussed included policy and regulatory reforms, finance and investment, sustainable supply chains, standards and certification, as well as capacity building.
The two sides also exchanged views on the development of eco-industrial parks, with Johari pointing to Malaysia's existing industrial infrastructure as a foundation for the transition.
"With approximately 600 industrial parks across the country, Malaysia is well positioned to adopt circular economy practices, beginning with our halal parks as a practical first step towards building a more sustainable and high-value industrial ecosystem," he said.
A circular economy model seeks to minimise waste and maximise resource use by keeping materials in circulation for as long as possible, in contrast to the conventional linear model of producing, using and discarding.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Turnout crosses 63pc as more than 1.69 million voters cast ballots before polls close
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.69 million voters, or 63.13 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 4pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC).
The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state.
At 3pm, the Semarang state constituency recorded the highest voter turnout at 66.1 per cent, followed by Sri Medan at 64.4 per cent and Penawar at 64.2 per cent.
Penggaram recorded the lowest turnout at 48.8 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 49 per cent and Perling at 50.3 per cent.
The EC said the turnout figures were shared through its official Facebook page.
For comparison, voter turnout at the same time during the 15th Johor state election stood at 45.39 per cent, with 1,168,809 out of more than 2.57 million registered voters having cast their ballots.
Polling will continue until 6pm today, after which vote counting will begin, with results expected to be announced later tonight.

Title: Typhoon Bavi forces Malaysia Airlines to cancel Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai flights
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — Malaysia Airlines has announced additional flight cancellations involving the Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai route due to adverse weather conditions associated with Typhoon Bavi, following yesterday’s advisory on cancellations involving the Kuala Lumpur-Taipei route.
In a statement today, the national carrier said the affected flights on July 11 are MH386 (Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai), MH388 (Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai) and MH389 (Shanghai-Kuala Lumpur).
It said flight MH387 (Shanghai-Kuala Lumpur), scheduled for July 12, has also been cancelled.
Yesterday, Malaysia Airlines announced the cancellation of flights MH366 (Kuala Lumpur-Taipei) and MH367 (Taipei-Kuala Lumpur), originally scheduled to operate on July 11, due to Typhoon Bavi.
The airline said it is closely monitoring developments and will continue to provide updates on flight operations as the weather situation evolves.
It advised passengers to update their contact details via the “My Booking” feature on the Malaysia Airlines website or mobile application to receive timely updates via email and SMS.
“Affected passengers may request a refund or rebook their travel on the same sector at no additional cost within 30 days from the original flight date via the ‘My Booking’ portal, AskMH Live Chat or by contacting the airline’s global contact centre.
“The safety of our passengers and crew remains of utmost importance to Malaysia Airlines,” it said.
Bavi is forecast to move northwest at 15-20 kilometres per hour and make landfall along the coast from Xiapu in Fujian to Wenling in Zhejiang on Saturday night, with its intensity weakening after landfall.
From Friday afternoon to Saturday afternoon, gales are expected in waters east of Taiwan, most parts of the East China Sea and waters near Diaoyu Dao, as well as along the coasts of Taiwan, Zhejiang, and Fujian. — Bernama

Title: New Bukit Kayu Hitam-Sadao route opens to ease Malaysia-Thailand border congestion
Article:
BUKIT KAYU HITAM, July 11 — The new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex in Kedah with the Sadao Customs, Immigration and Quarantine (CIQ) Complex in Thailand was opened to the public today.
The route, which opened at 6 am today, is expected to facilitate cross-border travel and ease congestion at the main entry point between Malaysia and Thailand, particularly during weekends and public holidays.
A Bernama check found traffic along the route to be smooth today, with most users expressing positive feedback on the opening of the new road.
One motorist, Mohd Faizal Ahmad, 42, from Penang, said the new route was better organised and offered greater convenience for Malaysians travelling to Thailand.
“Previously, traffic congestion was quite severe, especially during the school holidays. With this new route, I believe it will help reduce congestion and shorten travel time,” he said when met here today.
Lorry driver Wan Muhammad Shahid Wan Mohd Desa, 28, said the new road is wider and separates heavy and light vehicles more systematically when entering the Sadao CIQ, allowing for smoother traffic flow.
“Previously, heavy vehicles travelling from the Bukit Kayu Hitam ICQS to Danok shared the same route with other vehicles, resulting in severe congestion for traffic entering and leaving Thailand. We hope the new route will help reduce the problem,” he said.
For Thai national Mat Li Daman, 59, who frequently travels to Malaysia, the new Sadao CIQ is more spacious and comfortable, with improved facilities for documentation procedures, including vehicle entry declarations.
“The new building is much larger than the old, cramped checkpoint. I hope these facilities will benefit the people of both countries, who have long lived as neighbours,” he said.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his Thai counterpart, Anutin Charnvirakul, officiated the opening of the new road linking the Bukit Kayu Hitam ICQS with the Sadao CIQ.
It was developed to improve the efficiency of border operations, facilitate the movement of people and goods, and boost cross-border trade and tourism between Malaysia and Thailand. — Bernama

Title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 56.77pc by 3pm with more than 1.52 million ballots cast
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.52 million voters, or 56.77 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 3pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC).
The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state.
The Johor state election involves more than 2.6 million registered voters who are choosing representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.
Earlier, the EC reported that 1.31 million voters, or 48.91 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots as of 2pm.
At 1pm, more than 1.13 million voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had voted, with Semarang and Sedili recording the highest turnout at 50.5 per cent each, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent.
Polling will continue until 6pm today, after which vote counting will begin, with results expected to be announced later tonight.

Title: Thunderstorm warning issued for eight states and KL as heavy rain, strong winds expected
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to affect eight states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 5 pm today.
In a statement issued at 2 pm, MetMalaysia said the warning covers the whole of Melaka and Penang.
The affected areas in Kedah are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu.
The warning also covers Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim in Perak.
In Pahang, the affected districts are Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Bentong, Pekan and Rompin.
Similar weather conditions are forecast in Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat in Selangor; Jelebu in Negeri Sembilan; and Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang and Mersing in Johor.
MetMalaysia advised the public to remain alert and monitor its latest weather updates as conditions may change. — Bernama

Title: Pulau Besar becomes first Johor polling centre to close after 32 voters cast ballots
Article:
MERSING, July 11 — Balai Raya Pulau Besar became the first polling centre to close in today’s Johor state election.
Tenggaroh state constituency returning officer Jamil Hasni Abdullah said the centre, which had one channel and 32 registered voters, was closed at 11 am.
“The polling centres at Balai Raya Pulau Aur and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Pulau Pemanggil closed at noon, while Balai Raya Pulau Sibu and SK Pulau Tinggi closed at 2 pm,” he told Bernama.
All polling centres across the five islands cater to 690 registered voters.
The Election Commission reported that 42.16 per cent, or 1,130,980 of over 2.6 million voters, had cast their ballots as of 1 pm.
The polling process is scheduled to continue until 6 pm this evening.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Former Umno leader Isham Jalil applies to join PAS three years after party sacking
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 11 — Former Umno Supreme Council member Isham Jalil has applied to join PAS, nearly three years after he was sacked from the party.
Isham said he submitted his membership application yesterday, adding that he had decided to continue his political struggle with PAS, which he described as a “sanctuary” for Malay Muslims.
“PAS is also free of corruption,” he said in a Facebook post.
Isham was sacked from Umno in December 2023, months after he was elected to the party’s Supreme Council during its internal elections.
Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki had previously said Isham was removed for allegedly being disloyal, failing to follow party orders and directives, and not respecting party decisions.
Isham was previously a strong supporter of Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi but later became critical of Zahid and the party’s cooperation with DAP.
The former Umno leader’s move comes as PAS continues to strengthen its position within Perikatan Nasional (PN), which has positioned itself as an alternative political coalition at the federal level.

Title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 48.91pc by 2pm with more than 1.31 million ballots cast
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.31 million voters, or 48.91 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 2pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC).
The EC said the turnout figure was recorded as polling continued across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state.
The Johor state election involves more than 2.6 million registered voters who are choosing representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.
Earlier, the EC reported that 1,130,980 voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots as of 1pm.
The Semarang and Sedili state constituencies recorded the highest voter turnout at 50.5 per cent each at 1pm, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent.
Polling will continue until 6pm today, after which vote counting will begin, with results expected to be announced later tonight.

Title: AirBorneo to open ticket sales for Kuching-Singapore flights ahead of July 22 launch
Article:
KUCHING, July 11 — AirBorneo Airways will begin selling tickets for its inaugural international service between Kuching and Singapore on July 13, ahead of the launch of daily flights on July 22, which coincides with the Hari Sarawak celebration.
In a statement today, the Sarawak-owned airline said ticket sales would open at 10 am on July 13, with introductory all-inclusive one-way fares starting from RM399 for Economy Class and RM871 for Business Class.
“The new daily service between Kuching International Airport and Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 will strengthen Sarawak’s connectivity with one of the region’s key aviation and business hubs, while opening new opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and Singapore,” the statement said.
AirBorneo chief executive officer Megat Ardian Wira Mohd Aminuddin described the launch as a key milestone in the airline’s development.
He said the Kuching-Singapore service marked a significant step in AirBorneo’s growth, extending Sarawak’s air connectivity beyond Malaysia’s borders for the first time.
“Singapore is a vital regional and international hub, and this new route will create meaningful opportunities for trade, tourism and investment between Sarawak and the wider region.
“We remain committed to connecting people, businesses and opportunities through safe and reliable air travel. This milestone reflects our long-term ambition to expand AirBorneo’s international network in support of Sarawak’s economic and social development,” he said in the statement.
The airline said the inaugural flight’s timing was especially significant as it coincides with Hari Sarawak on July 22, when the state commemorates the anniversary of attaining self-government and celebrates its continued progress.
“The launch of AirBorneo’s first international route on this occasion reflects Sarawak’s growing aviation ambitions, reinforcing the state’s confidence in expanding its connectivity, trade and opportunities beyond Malaysia’s shores,” it added.
Under the flight schedule, Flight MY363 will depart Kuching daily at 6.50 pm and arrive at Singapore Changi Airport Terminal 4 at 8.25 pm, while the return service, Flight MY364, will depart Singapore at 9.15 pm and arrive in Kuching at 10.50 pm.
Tickets are available exclusively through AirBorneo’s official website and mobile application.
AirBorneo, which transitioned from MASwings in January this year, currently serves 21 destinations with a fleet comprising two Boeing 737-800 aircraft, eight ATR 72-500 aircraft and six DHC-6-400 Twin Otter aircraft, connecting major urban centres and remote highland communities across Sarawak. — Bernama

Title: Sarawak Immigration crackdown around Kuching, Kota Samarahan nets 24 foreign nationals
Article:
KUCHING, July 11 — The Sarawak Immigration Department detained 24 foreign nationals during Op Minyak, Op Selera, and Op Mahir, which were carried out at seven locations around Kuching and Kota Samarahan yesterday.
In a statement issued today, the department said the six-hour operation, which began at 9 am, was conducted following public complaints and prior intelligence gathered by the authorities.
Inspections focused on heavy machinery workshops in Kampung Semeba and Kampung Sudat in Kuching, as well as eateries and premises suspected of conducting online gambling activities in Muara Tuang, Kota Samarahan.
Several scrap metal and second-hand goods premises around Kota Samarahan were also inspected to ensure compliance with the country’s immigration laws.
A total of 24 foreign nationals were detained on suspicion of committing offences under Section 6(1)© and Section 15(1)© of the Immigration Act 1959/63 (Act 155). In addition, one witness summons was issued to assist with further investigations into the case, the department said.
The department also urged the public to channel information regarding the presence of undocumented immigrants or any unlawful activities to the authorities for further action. — Bernama

Title: LPT crash: More than 30 motorcycles believed involved in illegal racing, says police
Article:
KUANTAN, July 11 — A total of 20 people injured in an accident at Km246.4 of the East Coast Expressway (LPT) early today received treatment at two hospitals here.
District police chief ACP Ashari Abu Samah said 18 victims were treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), while two others received treatment at Sultan Ahmad Shah Medical Centre here.
“Further investigations revealed that more than 30 motorcycles, believed to have been racing, were involved in the accident that claimed four lives,” he said when contacted.
Earlier, Ashari said the crash, which occurred at about 1 am, claimed the lives of Che Mohd Sufian Che Ghani, 40, Muhammad Hafiz Al Hakim Mazlan, 33, and Mohd Aizat Husni, 33, who were all riding Yamaha RXZ motorcycles. They died at the scene.
He said another victim, Muhammad Safawi Md Noh, 24, died while receiving treatment at HTAA.
The accident is believed to have occurred when a motorcycle hit the rear of a car and fell onto the right lane, resulting in a chain-reaction collision involving several other motorcyclists.
Meanwhile, Che Mohd Sufian’s wife, Faridah Mat Asri, 39, said she was informed about the incident by a friend at about 1.30 am.
The mother of four said her husband and several friends had travelled to Kuantan from Terengganu, among others, to enjoy a meal at a popular traditional Pahang food restaurant in the city.
“The last time I saw him was yesterday afternoon, before I took our children to the beach. The last message I received from him was at midnight, and after that there were no more messages,” she told reporters when met at HTAA. — Bernama

Title: Johor polls: Voter turnout hits 42pc by 1pm, ahead of previous state election pace
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 1.13 million voters, or 42.16 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm today in the 16th Johor state election, according to the Election Commission (EC).
In a Facebook post, the EC said the Semarang and Sedili state constituencies recorded the highest voter turnout at 50.5 per cent each, followed by Tanjung Surat at 48.6 per cent.
Penggaram registered the lowest turnout at 32.2 per cent, followed by Bentayan at 32.6 per cent and Simpang Jeram at 35.2 per cent.
The turnout surpassed the pace recorded at the same point during the 15th Johor state election, when 888,605 voters, or 34.51 per cent of the electorate, had cast their ballots by 1pm.
Polling, involving more than 2.6 million registered voters across 56 state constituencies, continues until 6pm today.
Counting of votes will begin after polling closes, with results expected later tonight.

Title: Johor polls: IGP says no candidates implicated in 90 election-related police reports
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Police received a total of 90 reports across Johor during the 14-day campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, which ended at midnight yesterday.
Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said none of the reports involved election candidates or senior leaders of the contesting political parties.
Of the total, he said, 25 investigation papers had been opened to facilitate further action in accordance with the law.
He said most of the reports were related to acts of mischief, including the damaging, removal and burning of political party flags.
“At this stage, all the investigation papers are still being processed to identify those responsible,” he told reporters after visiting the polling centre at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Taman Molek today.
Also present to monitor the voting process were Internal Security and Public Order Department director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri and Johor Police Chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad.
Commenting on polling day, Mohd Khalid said the voting process involving more than 2.7 million registered voters had proceeded smoothly, with no untoward incidents reported so far.
To ensure public safety and maintain order, he said 14,000 police officers and personnel had been deployed across all 56 state constituencies in Johor.
“The deployment includes 5,600 officers and personnel from contingents nationwide who have been assigned to assist with election duties,” he said.
He also urged voters with complaints or grievances related to the election process to lodge reports with the police to enable the Election Commission and other relevant agencies to take prompt action.
Polling, involving more than 2.7 million registered voters, will continue until 6 p.m. today following the conclusion of the 14-day campaign period at midnight yesterday.
He also urged voters with complaints or grievances related to the election process to lodge reports with the police to enable the Election Commission and other relevant agencies to take prompt action.
Polling, involving more than 2.7 million registered voters, will continue until 6 pm today following the conclusion of the 14-day campaign period at midnight yesterday.
For the latest news on the 16th Johor State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Heavy rain and thunderstorms lash Johor, but voters brave the weather to keep polling on track
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — Heavy rain and thunderstorms across Johor since midday today have not dampened the spirit of voters turning out for the 16th Johor state election.
A Bernama check at several polling centres in Muar found continuous rain since morning affecting Sekolah Jenis Kebangsaan (SJK) Cina Limbong and Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Sri Muar.
Some voters were seen wearing raincoats while others carried umbrellas as they made their way to polling centres, determined to fulfil their duty despite the unfavourable weather.
In Johor Bahru, similar weather conditions at SMK Bandar Uda Utama also failed to deter voters from playing their part, with polling centres remaining calm and orderly.
According to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), thunderstorms are forecast to hit several areas in Johor this afternoon, including Iskandar Puteri, Johor Bahru, Kluang, Kota Tinggi, Kulai, Labis, Mersing, Pasir Gudang, Segamat and Simpang Renggam.
However, the weather is expected to improve by tonight, with no rain forecast for most areas.
Earlier, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun told a press conference that rain affecting several areas, including Muar, Kluang and Johor Bahru, was expected to have a slight impact on voter turnout this afternoon.
Due to the rainy weather, the EC anticipates some disruption to the turnout rate, but still projects it could reach around 65 to 70 per cent.
Johor takes centre stage today as more than 2.6 million registered voters head to the polls to choose their representatives for 56 state assembly seats in the 16th Johor state election.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, please visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Smooth traffic at Johor Causeway, Second Link and main JB roads on polling day
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 – Traffic conditions on several major routes in Johor were reported to be free-flowing to moderate on polling day despite an increase in the number of vehicles following the return of voters from Singapore.
Checks at the main south-bound routes at the Skudai toll plaza and Kulai toll plaza showed a smooth traffic flow towards the Johor Bahru city centre.
By late morning, no unusual congestion were reported despite the expected higher traffic volume earlier in the day.
The same situation was also recorded on the Johor Causeway, where vehicle movement from Singapore to Johor Bahru city at the Sultan Iskandar Building (BSI) was reported to be at moderate flow towards the afternoon.
Traffic flow from the republic was reported to be heavy at 8am, but gradually eased by late morning.
On the Second Link (LinKedua) Crossing, via the the Sultan Abu Bakar Complex (KSAB), traffic flow was reported at moderate flow with vehicles moving consistently without significant disruption.
Despite the increase in vehicles entering the Johor Bahru city centre, traffic was reported to be at a moderate flow towards the afternoon. July 11, 2026. — Picture by Ben Tan
Last Wednesday, the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) announced that their personnel will operate at maximum capacity at both the BSI and KSAB to facilitate the return of voters from Singapore for the Johor state election today.
Among the initiatives included opening dedicated lanes, activating hybrid counters and implementing contra-flow lanes to ensure smooth traffic flow and traveller movement at the country’s two main land entry points.
The advance planning was seen to assist in the smooth traffic flow at Johor’s main entry points, ensuring an efficient and orderly return journey to voters.
Previously, the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expected more than 300,000 voters from outside of Johor to return to fulfill their responsibilities in this election.
The Johor state election will see 172 candidates contesting 56 state assembly seats, with polling scheduled until 6pm today.

Title: Johor polls: More than 709,000 voters cast ballots by 11am as turnout hits 26.43pc
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — More than 709,000 voters, or 26.43 per cent of registered electors, had cast their ballots as of 11am today in the 16th Johor state election, Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said.
Ramlan said the turnout figure was recorded after polling began at 8am across 1,076 polling centres involving 4,889 polling streams throughout the state.
The Johor state election sees more than 2.6 million registered voters casting their ballots to elect representatives for 56 State Legislative Assembly seats contested by 172 candidates.
Earlier, the EC recorded a voter turnout of 7.97 per cent, or 213,878 voters, as of 9am.
The voting process is proceeding smoothly, with voters continuing to arrive at polling centres across Johor to fulfil their civic responsibility.

Title: No ban on shorts at polling stations, says EC after voter allegedly stopped in Skudai
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 11 — The Election Commission (EC) has clarified that there is no official dress code preventing voters from wearing shorts to polling stations, after an alleged incident involving a voter at a Skudai polling centre.
EC deputy chairman Azmi Sharom told
Malaysiakini
that the only restriction imposed was on clothing displaying political party logos or candidate branding.
“No such dress code. The only restriction is clothes with party logo or candidate,” he was quoted as saying.
The clarification came after a voter was allegedly barred from entering the polling station for the Skudai state seat at SMK Taman Tun Aminah for wearing shorts.
Photos circulating on Facebook showed a man in a yellow T-shirt and above-the-knee khaki shorts standing outside the school, with a caption claiming he was not allowed to enter the polling centre to vote.
